POW – More favorable Qc mechanism strengthens outlook for NT3 and 4 – Update

Company Note 30/06/2026    30

Share

  • We reiterate our ADD rating with 16.9% upside. We raise our target price by 18.9% compared with our previous report.
  • The higher target price primarily reflects a higher DCF valuation and the roll forward of our valuation base to mid-2026.
  • POW is currently trading at 1.1x P/B, below the regional peer average of 1.4x, which we consider attractive given the relatively positive outlook for its domestic gas-fired and LNG power.

Financial Highlights

  • 1Q26 net profit surged 169.6% YoY to VND1.2tn (USD46.2mn), driven by a wider gross margin.
  • We forecast 2026 NP to edge up 2.8% YoY, supported by higher electricity output despite weaker hydropower earnings from a high 2025 base.
  • We expect 2027 NP to grow a further 8.7% YoY, mainly driven by margin improvement following the application of a higher Qc level for LNG.

Investment Thesis

Supportive power-sector backdrop strengthens POW’s role in national power system

Electricity demand recovered in early 2026, with power consumption rising nearly 9% YoY in 5M26, supported by resilient manufacturing activity and hotter weather. EVN’s financial position also improved significantly, with 2025 net profit exceeding VND50.5tn (USD1.94bn), fully eliminating its previously substantial accumulated losses. This should support a more favorable payment and power-dispatch environment. With its current generation portfolio, POW is well positioned to benefit from rising demand for stable power sources during periods of peak system load.

Proposed higher LNG Qc strengthens the outlook for NT3 and 4

NT3 and 4 are expected to become POW’s largest revenue-growth contributor over 2026-30. A potential increase in minimum LNG Qc from 65% to 75%, alongside an extension of the application period from 10 to 15 years, would improve fixed-cost recovery and support margins. We forecast NT3 and 4 output at 6.3 billion/7.3 billion kWh in 2026/27, contributing 38%/41% of POW’s total revenue.

Coal-fired power supports near-term earnings as El Niño conditions prevail

VA1 should remain an important earnings contributor in 2026, supported by stronger thermal-power dispatch amid rising electricity demand and prevailing El Niño conditions. We forecast output at 6.3 billion kWh, up 5.6% YoY, while EVN’s foreign-exchange compensation provides additional revenue support. VA1 is expected to contribute around 21% of POW’s gross profit, partly offsetting weaker hydropower earnings.

Valuation remains attractive as earnings improve

We reiterate ADD and raise our target price to VND17,300/share (USD0.65/share). The increase mainly reflects higher 2026/27 EPS forecasts following upward revisions to Qc assumptions for NT1/NT2 and our assumption of 75% LNG Qc from 2027. We maintain our target P/B at 1.10x.

Read the full report: Here
